summary: Extract useStages and useSubStages shared hooks
problem_statement: Stage and sub-stage data fetching is duplicated inline across multiple components. Each component independently queries, caches, and error-handles the same data, creating maintenance overhead and inconsistency. A shared hook for each resource eliminates this duplication and provides a single point of control for fetching logic.
in_scope: Create a useStages hook that encapsulates stage data fetching, caching, loading, and error state
Create a useSubStages hook that encapsulates sub-stage data fetching, caching, loading, and error state
Replace all inline stage/sub-stage fetching in existing components with the new shared hooks
Ensure hooks use the typed apiRequest wrapper if P3-R2-09 is complete, or a typed fetch pattern otherwise
out_of_scope: Changing the API endpoints or response shapes for stages/sub-stages
Adding new data fetching capabilities beyond what currently exists inline
Refactoring other data fetching patterns not related to stages/sub-stages
requirements: A useStages hook exists and returns typed stage data with loading and error states
A useSubStages hook exists and returns typed sub-stage data with loading and error states
No inline stage/sub-stage fetching remains outside the shared hooks
All components using the hooks behave identically to the pre-refactor versions
acceptance_criteria: All stage/sub-stage data fetching uses shared hooks (useStages, useSubStages) with no inline duplication for those resources outside the hooks
Existing component behaviour is preserved exactly
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
dependencies: P2-R2-05 — 'any' removal ensures the hooks can be built with proper types
P2-R2-07 — domain entity types (Stage, SubStage) should be defined
risks: Components may use slightly different fetch patterns (different query keys, refetch intervals) that need to be unified carefully
implementation_notes: If the project uses TanStack Query (React Query), the hooks should wrap useQuery with the appropriate query key and fetch function. Export hooks from a shared hooks directory. Ensure the hooks accept parameters needed by different consumers (e.g. filtering by parent ID for sub-stages).
